## [00:00 - 03:04]

This is a pubcast shot. Wishi washi. I'm John Loomer. Let's go. So my most common answer to people who ask me questions related to metrics and whether they're good or bad is it depends and I fully understand that I'm probably frustrated a lot of people with my seemingly wishy washy answers but the truth is that there are very few if any absolutes when we're talking about this stuff. The factors that create context for the evaluation of your results really matter a lot. So when someone asks what is a good CPC well depends on what you're promoting depends on the audience you're targeting it depends on the industry depends ultimately on the value of that click to you or I'm asked what is a high CPM well depends on the season it depends on the audience size it depends on the competition it depends on how likely someone is to act when you reach them. What's a good cost per conversion you know the million dollar question well depends on the price of that product it depends on your cost to produce that product depends on the warmth of the audience expectation of conversion. Same thing with cost per lead what's the likelihood that lead is going to convert and if they do what is the value of that conversion. I mean that's a huge difference between a free e-book that you know maybe worth a few dollars to you and someone who could spend thousands with you because the sales person is going to talk to them. So all these things depend but you need to understand the value of the action you're trying to accomplish understand the ceiling cost that you can withstand and still be profitable and then the end be realistic is your product expensive well don't expect a one dollar cost per conversion. Do you have a large built-in audience or you focus just on cold people right now is your brand well known and respected or do you need to build that trust so in the end embrace it depends but ultimately you got to understand the factors that contribute to discovering the answers for yourself.
